subject: Ecology
title: Plant-Soil Feedback
description: A quick, practical guide to Plant-Soil Feedback.

hook.text: Plants change the soil they grow in—and that changed soil affects the next plant.

buildup.text: Some plants enrich soil with nitrogen or organic matter, helping future generations.

discovery.text: Others accumulate pathogens in soil over time, making the same spot worse for their own kind.

twist.text: Negative soil feedback explains why monocultures fail—pests build up until yields crash.

climax.text: Crop rotation exploits soil feedback: each crop resets the pathogens of the previous one.

punchline.text: Plants rewrite the soil. The soil rewrites them.

quiz.question: Why do monocultures often fail over time?
options: Pests and pathogens build up in the soil
